dhp1:1: Phenomena are preceded by the heart,
dhp1:2: ruled by the heart, made of the heart.
dhp1:6: as the wheel of the cart, the track of the ox that pulls it.
dhp2:2: preceded by the heart, ruled by the heart, made of the heart.
dhp2:6: like a shadow that never leaves.
dhp4:3: for those who don’t brood on this,
dhp6:2: that we’re here on the verge of perishing,
dhp7:1: One who stays focused on the beautiful,
dhp7:6: as the wind, a weak tree.
dhp8:1: One who stays focused on the foul,
dhp8:2: is restrained with regard to the senses,
dhp8:6: as the wind, a mountain of rock.
dhp9:3: devoid of truthfulness & self-control, puts on the ochre robe,
dhp10:1: But he who is free of depravity
dhp10:3: endowed with truthfulness & self-control, well-established in the precepts,
dhp11:1: Those who regard non-essence as essence
dhp12:1: But those who know essence as essence,
dhp15:1: Here he grieves he grieves hereafter.
dhp15:2: In both worlds the wrong-doer grieves.
dhp15:4: seeing the corruption of his deeds.
dhp16:1: Here he rejoices he rejoices hereafter.
dhp16:2: In both worlds the merit-maker rejoices.
dhp16:4: seeing the purity of his deeds.
dhp17:1: Here he’s tormented he’s tormented hereafter.
dhp17:2: In both worlds the wrong-doer’s tormented.
dhp17:3:
He’s tormented at the thought,
‘I’ve done wrong.’
dhp17:4: Having gone to a bad destination, he’s tormented all the more.
dhp18:1: Here he delights he delights hereafter.
dhp18:2: In both worlds the merit-maker delights.
dhp18:3:
He delights at the thought,
‘I’ve made merit.’
dhp18:4: Having gone to a good destination, he delights all the more.
dhp19:1: If he recites many teachings, but
dhp19:2: –heedless man– doesn’t do what they say,
dhp19:3: like a cowherd counting the cattle of others,
dhp19:4: he has no share in the contemplative life.
dhp20:1: If he recites next to nothing
dhp20:2: but follows the Dhamma in line with the Dhamma;
dhp20:5: not clinging either here or hereafter:
dhp20:6: he has his share in the contemplative life.
